first_four_theorem_status.pass_condition: family patch separation remains above current large-inventory floor under scaffolded readout intervention
first_four_theorem_status.fail_condition: cross-family mixing rises to same-family band under scaffolded readout

first_four_theorem_status.pass_condition: reasoning-slice transport continues to beat readout-only contrast while preserving restricted overlap behavior
first_four_theorem_status.fail_condition: reasoning transport gain disappears or only survives under direct collapse

first_four_theorem_status.pass_condition: stage-conditioned transport exceeds stage-free readout under long-chain constraints
first_four_theorem_status.fail_condition: stage labels do not improve transport once family and reasoning slice are controlled

first_four_theorem_status.pass_condition: successor-aware transport preserves chain-local successor coherence better than chain-agnostic transport
first_four_theorem_status.fail_condition: successor-aware transport yields no coherence gain over chain-agnostic transport

summary.meaning: The first two theorems now have stronger survival support, while the new stage/successor theorems are provisionally supported and should become the next direct intervention targets.

verdict.core_answer: Long-chain constraints now give the project a four-theorem active survival frontier: two stronger survivors and two provisional but advancing survivors.
verdict.next_theory_target: convert the two provisional survivals into stricter pass/fail tests by strengthening stage and successor perturbations.
